programme.title: Love Nature
programme.desc: In Holywood, County Down, a piece of land has been transformed into an edible forest garden shaped by patience, dedication, and care for wildlife. Toby Demeuldre, who became deeply interested in ecology and rewilding after surviving a childhood brain tumour, has spent years restoring the land-digging ponds, creating habitats, and building a photography hide to observe the animals that now rely on it. With the support of his mother Jane-Louise, the garden stands as both a sanctuary for local wildlife and a deeply personal space of healing, showing how slowing down and nurturing nature can benefit us all. Exec Prods: Fintan Maguire/Catriona Doyle; High Rock Media (S1 Ep11)
Short films focussing on the people, places and projects protecting and restoring nature.

programme.title: The Motorway
programme.desc: The patrollers deal with a freak accident and a family in shock when a lorry's wheel comes off and destroys their car. Heavy rain sees floods, blocked drains and a high-speed aquaplaning disaster on the M42.
Patrollers deal with a freak accident and a family in shock when a lorry wheel comes off.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Following the work of the Firearms Squad. The team respond to reports of a shotgun changing hands in the suburbs. When the team swoops in, both buyer and seller are taken by surprise.
This episode follows the work of the Firearms Squad.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: Humberside Police get a 999 call from a member of the public following an HGV which is all over the road and driving very dangerously. In a dramatic live relay of events, the truck narrowly misses other cars and completely misses a roundabout. Can they stop it before it causes catastrophe? In Cleethorpes, a routine red light stop raises deeper suspicions - thedriver claims this was just an innocent mistake and she's never been in trouble before, but the only problem is that her car stinks of cannabis. Series Prod: Johnny Perks; Exec Prods: Zoë Callan/Dympna Jackson; Dirs: Sam Knowles/John Nichols/Phil Hirst/Emma Fraser; Purple Productions (S9 Ep3)
Ride along with the officers risking their lives policing Britain's most dangerous roads.

programme.title: The Curfew
programme.desc: Six-part crime thriller set in a society where all men live under The Women's Safety act, meaning they are bound by a strict curfew from 7pm to 7am every night, with their movements tracked 24 hours a day by an ankle tag. When a woman is brutally murdered in the night, maverick Detective Pamela Green is convinced only a man could have been responsible for the murder. Her theory is discredited among her peers: in a world where men are under curfew at night and their every move tracked by an ankle tag, it's not possible. Pamela, reluctantly partnered with eager rookie DC Eddie Wilson, sets about finding what happened to the victim. Starring Sarah Parish, Mandip Gill, Alexandra Burke, Lucy Benjmain and Anita Dobson. (Originally screened on Paramount+ with the title Curfew.) (Continues tomorrow.) (Ep1/6)

programme.title: Platoon 24/7: Preparing For War
programme.desc: It's summer in Bulford and 13 Platoon is reaching the end of its training. But ahead of them are the two biggest tests of their six months of training. First is Exercise Iron Storm, in which the platoon fight alongside tanks and artillery in a large-scale exercise across the Salisbury Plain Training Area. With their rifles and body armour equipped with a laser tracking system, this is the first time the platoon can kill - and be killed - in a battle simulation. Their second challenge is an endurance competition, staged on the inhospitable terrain of Dartmoor in Devon. Carrying 30kg of kit, the platoon must race non-stop over 45 kilometres while also undertaking a series of energy-sapping combat exercises. To pass their training the whole platoon must cross the finish line together. Can Platoon Commander Lieutenant Tom Drew and Corporal Jack Sherborne galvanise their soldiers one final time to get them across the finish line together as a band of brothers? (This is the 3rd and final - but retitled - episode of the series Platoon 24/7: Preparing for War.) Exec Prods: Martin Oxley/Jamie Balment; Dir: Will Lorimer; Frank Films and Motion Entertainment Production (S1 Ep3/3)
We follow six gruelling months as a platoon prepares for possible frontline deployment.
